blurb: SiriKit integrates third-party apps into Siri using the app extension model, with strict data minimization for user privacy. Before passing personal data to an app's SiriKit extension, Siri checks the app's user-granted permission settings: if the app lacks Contacts access, Siri passes only literal text for relationship references rather than resolved contact data. Custom vocabulary submitted by SiriKit-enabled apps is associated with a random identifier rather than the user's identity, and is purged when the app is deleted or Siri integration is disabled. Only the minimal data needed for the specific user intent is forwarded to the extension process.
